title: M36 T1 — capability taxonomy enum + shared-types scaffold
predecessor: M36 T0 SHIPPED 2026-04-21 (commit dc8d4d3). Plan-doc-only per Phase 5 M28 T0 precedent. Locked outcomes: 41 capabilities across 6 categories (engineering 10 + product 7 + design 5 + operations 7 + strategy 6 + support 6); case-sensitive lowercase_snake_case (Q1 resolved); frontmatter not DB (D16 reaffirmed); pack.json version bump 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 on backfill completion; subtractive categorization rubric (role.md body is canonical source, not title). Head-of-queue now advances to M36 T1 — first CODE task of Phase 6.
goal: Author the capability taxonomy enum + supporting type surface in packages/shared-types/src/capabilities.ts. This is the foundational scaffold that M36 T2 (parser validation), M36 T3+ (role.md backfill), and M37 (computeRoleFit v2) all consume. Implementation is surgical and data-driven — no logic beyond the type guard. Taxonomy strings MUST match the M36 T0 plan doc §5.1 verbatim; any variance is a plan-doc-compliance failure and requires a retrospective-driven amendment, not a silent drift.
acceptance: packages/shared-types/src/capabilities.ts NEW file exports: Capability (41-literal string-literal union), CAPABILITY_LIST (readonly tuple of 41 strings, order matches §5.1 category-by-category), CapabilityCategory ('engineering' | 'product' | 'design' | 'operations' | 'strategy' | 'support'), CAPABILITY_CATEGORY_MAP (readonly record — 6 keys, each value is a readonly Capability[]), isCapability(s: unknown): s is Capability (runtime type guard using CAPABILITY_LIST.includes)
Every capability string is lowercase_snake_case (§5.2); no capital letters, no hyphens, no dots, no trailing whitespace
Every Capability appears in exactly one category of CAPABILITY_CATEGORY_MAP (no orphans, no duplicates across categories)

The three representative capabilities pinned by the source-string-audit marker test are: engineering → 'backend_engineering'; product → 'product_management'; design → 'ux_design'. Literal matches; no variant strings
packages/shared-types/src/index.ts re-exports every public surface from capabilities.ts

NEW packages/shared-types/src/capabilities.test.ts contains at minimum: (a) count assertion (41), (b) per-category count assertions (6 assertions), (c) category-name pin (source-string audit), (d) three representative literal pins (source-string audit), (e) isCapability positive path on known string, (f) isCapability negative path on unknown string, (g) isCapability negative path on non-string input, (h) CAPABILITY_CATEGORY_MAP exhaustiveness (every Capability appears exactly once across all 6 category arrays)
Verification gate: pnpm -F @team-x/shared-types test green; pnpm -F @team-x/shared-types build green; repo-root pnpm typecheck clean (builds composite refs in lockstep with the shared-types package)
Unit test baseline moves 1169 → ~1184 (+~15). Lint 0 errors / ≤24 warnings preserved. E2E not re-run (no renderer surface)
No parser changes in T1 — capabilities field validation lands in M36 T2. No role.md edits — those start at M36 T3+. No IPC, no bus events, no settings keys
Zero dependencies on any Phase 5 milestone (M36 T1 scaffolds a greenfield surface in shared-types). No changes to existing shared-types files other than re-export additions in index.ts
commitTemplate: feat(m36-t1): M36 T1 — capability taxonomy enum + shared-types scaffold
notes: M36 T0 plan doc §5.1 lists all 41 capabilities verbatim. Copy the strings INTO capabilities.ts rather than retyping them — any transcription drift is caught by the category-count assertions and the source-string-audit literal pins. If drift is detected: STOP, re-read §5.1, correct in-place, do not amend §5.1.
Q1 case-sensitivity (lowercase_snake_case) is the ONE convention. A unit test MUST assert /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*[a-z0-9]$/ on every CAPABILITY_LIST entry to catch any capital-letter drift at CI time.

The six category NAMES are also source-string-audited: 'engineering' / 'product' / 'design' / 'operations' / 'strategy' / 'support'. Do not rename. If the category names need to change, that's a retrospective-driven amendment at the Phase level, not a silent T1 change.

CAPABILITY_LIST should be a TUPLE (as const) so TypeScript widens to string-literal union via typeof CAPABILITY_LIST[number]. Do not type it as string[] — that collapses the literal union back to string and every downstream consumer loses type safety.

CAPABILITY_CATEGORY_MAP must be Record<CapabilityCategory, readonly Capability[]> (or equivalent) so: (a) missing a category fails typecheck, (b) unknown category string fails typecheck, (c) runtime mutation is forbidden.

isCapability(s): don't use .includes for the fast path — use CAPABILITY_LIST.includes(s as Capability) only after typeof s === 'string'. Otherwise passing a non-string (e.g., undefined from a malformed role.md parse) throws at runtime.
After T1 lands: M36 T2 extends packages/role-schema/src/parser.ts to consume isCapability + CAPABILITY_LIST for validation. Keep T1 scope surgical so T2 can plug in without refactors.

Verification recipe: Node ABI rebuild → pnpm test (1169 → ~1184) → pnpm typecheck (composite refs rebuild clean across 6 packages) → pnpm lint (0 errors / ≤24 warnings) → commit atomically → ledger commit. E2E NOT re-run in T1 (no renderer change).
